Package algorithm.gaaco

Class Population

java.lang.Object
algorithm.gaaco.Population
public final class Population extends Object
A mutable, ordered collection of Route objects representing one generation in the Genetic Algorithm.

Mutability contract

Individual routes within the population may be replaced via set(int, algorithm.Route), allowing the GA to build each new generation in-place without allocating a new population object for every internal step. The backing list is exposed as an unmodifiable view through getRoutes(); callers that need to iterate routes safely can use that accessor.

Sorting

sortByFitness() orders routes from highest fitness (best route) to lowest, placing the elite candidates at the front of the list — exactly where the GA expects to find them when it copies elite routes into the next generation unchanged.

  • Constructor Details

    • Population

      public Population(List<Route> routes)
      Constructs a population from the given route list.

      A defensive copy of routes is made; modifications to the caller's list after construction have no effect on this population.

      Parameters:
      routes - initial route list; must not be null or empty
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if routes is null or empty

  • Method Details

    • getRoutes

      public List<Route> getRoutes()
      Returns an unmodifiable view of the route list.

      Use get(int) and set(int, algorithm.Route) to access individual slots when a mutable reference to an element is required.

      Returns:
      unmodifiable ordered list of routes

    • get

      public Route get(int index)
      Returns the route at the given index.
      Parameters:
      index - zero-based position in the population
      Returns:
      route at that position
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if index is out of range

    • set

      public void set(int index, Route route)
      Replaces the route at the given index.

      Used by the GA to install crossed-over and mutated routes into the population without creating a new population object.

      Parameters:
      index - zero-based position to replace
      route - replacement route; must not be null
      Throws:
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if index is out of range
      NullPointerException - if route is null

    • size

      public int size()
      Returns the number of routes in this population.
      Returns:
      population size (≥ 1)

    • sortByFitness

      public void sortByFitness()
      Sorts the population in descending fitness order (best route first).

      After this call, the elite routes occupy the lowest indices and the weakest routes occupy the highest indices — the layout that GeneticAlgorithm relies on when it preserves elites and replaces the tail of the population by crossover.

    • getBest

      public Route getBest()
      Returns the route with the highest fitness value in this population.

      Works correctly regardless of sort state: iterates the entire list and picks the global maximum. For a sorted population the result is always get(0).

      Returns:
      best route in the population

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
